title: Scaling Laws for Reward Model Overoptimization in Direct Alignment Algorithms

methodology.experiments: Experiments across different model scales and hyperparameters
methodology.unification: Unifying different recent methods under the DAA framework

main_findings: DAAs exhibit reward over-optimization trends similar to traditional RLHF methods
Performance degradation patterns at different KL-divergence budgets
Performance within a single epoch is not always consistent for DAAs
The under-constrained nature of the optimization problem in DAAs explains these phenomena

introduction.context: Advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) and their applications
introduction.problem: Goodhart's Law as a core issue with RLHF, leading to reward over-optimization
introduction.solution: Direct Alignment Algorithms (DAAs) as a new class of algorithms that bypass traditional RLHF pipeline
